faqs.q: What are the parts of the therapeutic alliance?
faqs.a: Researchers usually describe three parts: a warm bond between the person and therapist, agreement on the goals of therapy, and agreement on the tasks that will get them there. When those line up, the person feels safe enough to be honest.

faqs.q: Why does the therapeutic alliance matter?
faqs.a: Decades of research find that the quality of this relationship is one of the most reliable predictors of whether therapy works, often more than which specific method is used.

faqs.q: Is the therapeutic alliance the same as being friends with your therapist?
faqs.a: No. It's a professional relationship with clear boundaries that keep the focus on the person's growth, and it's about trust and working as a team, not just liking your therapist.
